message:
fix(html_export): re-attach code copy buttons after decrypt (m99lp)

Closes coding_agent_session_search-m99lp. Unencrypted HTML
exports run generate_init_js once after DOMContentLoaded and
attach .copy-code-btn elements to every <pre><code> block. Encrypted
exports decrypt the bundle into #conversation.innerHTML AFTER
init has already run, and Crypto.decrypt only re-initialises
ToolCalls, ToolPopovers, Search, and WorldClass — so the freshly-
inserted code blocks in a decrypted export never received copy
buttons, even though the styling and handlers for them were
already present in the emitted CSS/JS. Observable user-facing
parity gap: unencrypted exports had working copy buttons,
encrypted exports did not.

Fix: extract the copy-button attachment into a named, idempotent
helper `__cassAttachCodeCopyButtons()` that both init paths can
call:

- Idempotency guard: `if (!pre || pre.querySelector('.copy-code-btn'))
  return;` — re-running on an already-decorated <pre> is a no-op,
  so the initial init + the post-decrypt re-run don't double up.
- The helper is emitted OUTSIDE the inits.join(...) block as a
  standalone top-level `const __cassAttachCodeCopyButtons = () => { … }`
  in the generated module so Crypto.decrypt can see it from
  inside its own try-block on the typeof __cassAttachCodeCopyButtons
  === 'function' guard.
- generate_init_js now pushes
  `try { __cassAttachCodeCopyButtons(); } catch (e) {
      console.error('Code copy init failed', e); }`
  into the existing inits vec alongside the other init calls, so
  the initial DOMContentLoaded path uses the same helper the
  decrypt path does — single source of truth.
- Crypto.decrypt adds `if (typeof __cassAttachCodeCopyButtons ===
  'function') __cassAttachCodeCopyButtons();` next to the existing
  ToolCalls / ToolPopovers / Search / WorldClass re-inits, so
  decrypt-produced code blocks get the same treatment as
  DOMContentLoaded-produced ones.

Golden updates:

- tests/golden/html_export/basic_export.html.golden picks up the
  new helper declaration + the try/catch init invocation (4 new
  lines of JS).
- tests/golden/html_export/encrypted_export.html.golden picks up
  the same helper declaration + the try/catch init + the
  post-decrypt call inside Crypto.decrypt (7 new lines).

.beads/issues.jsonl closes m99lp with a reference to this commit;
.beads/last-touched advances.

Also picks up a trivial #[rustfmt::skip]-style reflow of the
test_pane_interaction_bundle assertion block (one chain of
`.contains(..)` folded into a multi-line assert! by rustfmt);
unrelated to the copy-button fix but landed in the same working
tree.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-m7xrw","title":"[MEDIUM] mock-finder: analytics Track B repair path is still unavailable","description":"Mock-code-finder finding in prod paths.

Locations:
- src/analytics/validate.rs:170/174 classify Track B and some cross-track validation failures as `TrackAllRebuildUnavailable`.
- src/analytics/validate.rs:185 reports `Track B or cross-track repair needs a track-all rebuild path that is not implemented yet`.
- src/lib.rs:4562 still documents that Track B rebuild will be wired later, and src/lib.rs:4826 only defers Track B repair decisions.

What is incomplete:
`cass analytics validate --fix` can detect Track B/cross-track drift, but the repair planner cannot repair those failures automatically. This remains true even though closed bead z9fse.13 was intended to make analytics rebuilds coherent across Track A and Track B. The current code only calls `FrankenStorage::rebuild_analytics()` for Track A and `rebuild_token_daily_stats()` exists only as a lower-level helper for rebuilding `token_daily_stats` from the existing `token_usage` ledger.

Suggested completion:
Add an explicit all-track analytics rebuild/repair path. At minimum, route repairable Track B rollup failures through `rebuild_token_daily_stats()` when `token_usage` is intact; for cross-track or missing-ledger drift, implement a Track B rebuild from canonical messages/conversations/agents using `extract_tokens_for_agent`, recompute `token_usage`, `token_daily_stats`, and conversation summaries transactionally, then update `cass analytics rebuild/validate --fix` JSON to report `tracks_rebuilt: ["a","b"]`. Add regression tests for `track_b.has_data` and `cross_track.drift` that prove `--fix` repairs instead of deferring.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:20:44.748710025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:27:20.905394867Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:27:20.905045152Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 3abc810b: Track B repair path wired end-to-end. New RepairKind::RebuildTrackB variant; classify_repair_kind() routes repairable track_b.* checks (has_data, grand_total_match, tool_calls_match, non_negative_counters) to RebuildTrackB while ledger/infrastructure failures (tables_exist, agents_table_missing, query_exec) correctly stay TrackAllRebuildUnavailable. src/lib.rs --fix exec loop calls storage.rebuild_token_daily_stats() for RebuildTrackB decisions; on failure defers with actionable hint. --fix JSON envelope gains top-level tracks_rebuilt field reporting BTreeSet-ordered subset of ['a','b']. Replaced prior unavailable-for-all test with pair that pins data-drift→RebuildTrackB and tables-missing→TrackAllRebuildUnavailable. Cross-track drift classification deliberately unchanged (full canonical replay is ibuuh.29/z9fse.13 scope). Verified: 176/176 analytics tests pass including 22 validate::* tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-m7y","title":"P6.2 Apply path mappings at display time","description":"# P6.2 Apply path mappings at INGEST time

## Overview
**IMPORTANT CHANGE:** Workspace path rewriting must happen at ingest time, not display time.
This is critical so that workspace FILTERS work consistently across local and remote sources.

## Why Ingest-Time (not Display-Time)
If a user searches with `--workspace=/Users/me/projects/myapp`, they expect to find:
- Local sessions from `/Users/me/projects/myapp`
- Remote sessions where the ORIGINAL path was `/home/user/projects/myapp`

If we only rewrite at display-time, the filter won't match the remote sessions because
they're stored with the original path. Ingest-time rewriting ensures filter consistency.

## Implementation Details

### Rewrite During Normalization
In the indexer, after connector produces NormalizedConversation:
```rust
fn apply_workspace_rewrite(
    conv: &mut NormalizedConversation,
    source: &SourceDefinition,
) {
    if let Some(ref workspace) = conv.workspace {
        let rewritten = source.rewrite_path(workspace);
        if rewritten != *workspace {
            // Store original in metadata for audit/display
            conv.metadata.insert(
                "workspace_original".into(),
                serde_json::Value::String(workspace.clone())
            );
            conv.workspace = Some(rewritten);
        }
    }
}
```

### Storage of Original Path
In SQLite, add `workspace_original` column (nullable):
```sql
ALTER TABLE conversations ADD COLUMN workspace_original TEXT;
```

In Tantivy, optionally add `workspace_original` as STORED (not indexed):
```rust
schema_builder.add_text_field("workspace_original", STORED);
```

### CLI/TUI Display
When displaying results, show the rewritten (local) path by default.
If user wants to see original, add `--show-original-paths` flag or
show on hover/detail view:
```
Workspace: /Users/me/projects/myapp
           (originally /home/user/projects/myapp on work-laptop)
```

### Robot Output
Include both for machine consumption:
```json
{
  "workspace": "/Users/me/projects/myapp",
  "workspace_original": "/home/user/projects/myapp"
}
```

## Filter Behavior After Rewrite
With ingest-time rewriting:
- `cass search --workspace=/Users/me/projects/myapp` finds BOTH local and remote sessions
- Grouping by workspace works correctly across machines
- TUI workspace filter shows unified list

## Dependencies
- Requires P6.1 (mapping rules defined)
- Requires P2.2 (indexer orchestration, where rewrite happens)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Workspace rewritten during indexing, not display
- [ ] Original path preserved in metadata/column
- [ ] Workspace filters work across sources
- [ ] Robot output includes both paths
- [ ] Re-indexing applies new mappings to existing sessions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedRiver","created_at":"2025-12-16T06:09:36.964081Z","updated_at":"2026-01-02T13:44:58.380515Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T07:39:05.921485Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-m7y","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-m7y","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-alb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-m7y","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-rv8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-m86q","title":"[P1] Opt 4: Output-Field Laziness (Skip Unused Stored Fields)","description":"# Optimization 4: Output-Field Laziness

## Problem Statement

Profiling shows `StoreReader::read_block` is a **top hotspot** in CLI-per-search mode. For `--fields minimal` or `--robot-format sessions`, we don't need `content`, `snippet`, or `preview` fields - yet we always load them.

### CPU Profile Evidence (from perf)
```
3.44% tantivy::store::reader::StoreReader::read_block (stored field reads)
```

### Use Cases Where Full Fields Are Unnecessary
1. `--fields minimal`: Only needs `source_path`, `line_number`, `agent`
2. `--robot-format sessions`: Only needs unique `source_path` values
3. `--count-only`: Only needs hit count, no fields at all
4. TUI list view: Only needs title/preview, not full content

## Proposed Solution

Thread "requested fields" through the search pipeline and skip stored field hydration for unrequested fields.

### Implementation Location
- File: `src/search/query.rs` (SearchClient::search)
- File: `src/search/tantivy.rs` (hit hydration logic)
- File: `src/lib.rs` (output formatting)

### Implementation Strategy

1. **Add FieldMask enum**:
```rust
#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
pub enum FieldMask {
    Full,           // All fields
    Minimal,        // source_path, line_number, agent only
    SessionsOnly,   // Just source_path for deduplication
    CountOnly,      // No fields, just count
}
```

2. **Modify SearchClient::search signature**:
```rust
pub fn search(
    &self,
    query: &str,
    limit: usize,
    field_mask: FieldMask,  // NEW PARAMETER
) -> Result<SearchResult>
```

3. **Conditional field hydration**:
```rust
// In hit hydration
match field_mask {
    FieldMask::Full => {
        // Load all stored fields (current behavior)
    }
    FieldMask::Minimal => {
        // Skip content, snippet, preview
        // Only load: source_path, line_number, agent, message_id
    }
    FieldMask::SessionsOnly => {
        // Only load source_path
    }
    FieldMask::CountOnly => {
        // Don't hydrate at all, just count matches
    }
}
```

## Isomorphism Proof

This optimization preserves correctness because:
1. **Ranking is unchanged**: Scores come from Tantivy BM25, not stored fields
2. **Hit ordering is unchanged**: Order determined by query execution, not hydration
3. **Field independence**: Stored fields have no interdependencies
4. **Output correctness**: Only requested fields matter; others can be omitted

### Formal Property
If a field is not requested, not computing it cannot affect:
- Ranking/ordering (computed from Tantivy scores)
- Other fields (no dependencies between stored fields)

## Expected Impact

| Scenario | Before | After | Improvement |
|----------|--------|-------|-------------|
| `--fields minimal` | 100% stored field reads | ~20% stored field reads | ~5x less I/O |
| `--robot-format sessions` | Full hydration | Path-only | ~10x less I/O |
| Cold-open CLI search | Dominated by stored field reads | Much reduced | Noticeable |

The actual latency improvement depends on:
- How much of the 3.44% hotspot is skippable
- I/O vs CPU ratio on target hardware
- Index file layout (block alignment)

## Implementation Notes

### Backward Compatibility
No external API changes needed. Internal refactor only. Default to `FieldMask::Full` for existing callers.

### Threading Through the Stack
The field mask needs to propagate:
1. CLI parsing (`--fields minimal`) → FieldMask
2. `run_search()` → `SearchClient::search(field_mask)`
3. `SearchClient` → Tantivy hit hydration

### Already-Shipped Related Work
The sessions output short-circuit (`src/lib.rs:3672`) already optimizes the *output* side by computing `BTreeSet<&str>` of source_paths. This optimization extends that pattern to the *input* side (stored field reads).

## Verification Plan

1. **Metamorphic test**: Same hit ordering for Full vs Minimal modes
2. **Field presence test**: Minimal mode returns correct fields, others are absent
3. **Benchmark**: Measure stored field read reduction with criterion

## Rollback Strategy

Environment variable `CASS_LAZY_FIELDS=0` to:
- Always hydrate all fields regardless of request
- Useful for debugging missing field issues

## Dependencies

- None (independent optimization)
- Can be implemented in parallel with P0 vector optimizations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-10T03:01:12.249545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T07:52:18.139055Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T07:52:18.139055Z","close_reason":"Added FieldMask minimal metamorphic test (ordering + omitted fields) in tests/search_filters.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-mbei","title":"[Task] Opt 7.2: Implement SQLite ID caching","description":"## Objective
Implement HashMap-based caching for agent and workspace IDs during indexing.

## Implementation Details
```rust
use std::collections::HashMap;

pub struct IndexingCache {
    agent_ids: HashMap<String, i64>,
    workspace_ids: HashMap<String, i64>,
}

impl IndexingCache {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            agent_ids: HashMap::new(),
            workspace_ids: HashMap::new(),
        }
    }

    pub fn get_or_insert_agent(
        &mut self,
        tx: &Transaction,
        name: &str,
    ) -> Result<i64> {
        if let Some(&id) = self.agent_ids.get(name) {
            return Ok(id);
        }
        let id = ensure_agent(tx, name)?;
        self.agent_ids.insert(name.to_string(), id);
        Ok(id)
    }

    pub fn get_or_insert_workspace(
        &mut self,
        tx: &Transaction,
        name: &str,
    ) -> Result<i64> {
        if let Some(&id) = self.workspace_ids.get(name) {
            return Ok(id);
        }
        let id = ensure_workspace(tx, name)?;
        self.workspace_ids.insert(name.to_string(), id);
        Ok(id)
    }
}
```

## Integration Points
- Create cache at start of batch processing
- Pass cache through indexing call chain
- Clear cache at transaction boundaries (if needed)

## Thread Safety
- Cache is per-batch, single-threaded context
- No need for synchronization primitives

## Rollback
```rust
if std::env::var("CASS_SQLITE_CACHE").as_deref() == Ok("0") {
    // Bypass cache, use direct DB calls
}
```

## Parent Feature

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-mjid","title":"P1.4b: Non-Interactive Pages Config (Robot Mode)","description":"# P1.4b: Non-Interactive Pages Config (Robot Mode)

## Goal
Allow fully automated `cass pages` runs without interactive wizard input. Accept a JSON config file (or stdin) that defines export filters, encryption, bundle options, and deployment target. This is critical for CI/CD and scripted workflows.

## CLI Interface

```
# File-based
cass pages --config ./pages-config.json --json

# Stdin
cat pages-config.json | cass pages --config - --json

# Validate only
cass pages --config ./pages-config.json --validate-config
```

## Config Schema (high-level)

```json
{
  "filters": {
    "agents": ["claude-code", "codex"],
    "since": "30 days ago",
    "until": "2025-01-06",
    "workspaces": ["/path/one", "/path/two"],
    "path_mode": "relative"
  },
  "encryption": {
    "password": "env:EXPORT_PASSWORD",
    "generate_recovery": true,
    "generate_qr": true,
    "compression": "deflate",
    "chunk_size": 8388608
  },
  "bundle": {
    "title": "Team Archive",
    "description": "Encrypted cass export",
    "include_pwa": false,
    "include_attachments": false
  },
  "deployment": {
    "target": "github|cloudflare|local",
    "repo": "my-archive",
    "branch": "gh-pages",
    "output_dir": "./dist"
  }
}
```

## Requirements
- Full parity with wizard options
- Clear schema validation errors
- Support env:VAR resolution for secrets
- `--validate-config` prints validation result without performing export
- JSON output for automation

## Test Requirements

### Unit Tests
- schema validation (required/optional fields)
- env var resolution
- invalid values produce actionable errors

### Integration Tests
- run export with config fixture
- verify JSON output for CI

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/pages/config_input.rs
- src/cli/pages.rs (add --config, --validate-config)
- tests/pages_config_input.rs

## Exit Criteria
1. Non-interactive export works end-to-end
2. Config validation catches errors early
3.  {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-mlou","title":"P2.4: Key Slot Management","description":"# Key Slot Management

**Parent Phase:** coding_agent_session_search-yjq1 (Phase 2: Encryption)
**Depends On:** P2.2 (AES-256-GCM Encryption)
**Estimated Duration:** 2 days

## Goal

Implement the key slot system that allows multiple passwords/recovery secrets to unlock the same archive, enabling password rotation and sharing without re-encryption.

## Technical Approach

### Key Slot Structure

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct KeySlot {
    pub id: u32,
    pub slot_type: String,            // "password" or "recovery"
    pub kdf: String,                  // "argon2id" or "hkdf-sha256"
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    pub kdf_params: Option<KdfParams>,
    #[serde(with = "base64_bytes")]
    pub salt: [u8; 16],
    #[serde(with = "base64_bytes")]
    pub nonce: [u8; 12],
    #[serde(with = "base64_bytes")]
    pub wrapped_dek: Vec<u8>,         // 48 bytes (32B DEK + 16B tag)
}
```

### DEK Wrapping

```rust
/// Wrap DEK with KEK for a single slot
pub fn wrap_dek(
    dek: &[u8; 32],
    kek: &[u8; 32],
    export_id: &[u8; 16],
    slot_id: u32,
) -> Result<(Vec<u8>, [u8; 12]), CryptoError> {
    let cipher = Aes256Gcm::new(Key::from_slice(kek));
    let mut nonce = [0u8; 12];
    rand::thread_rng().fill_bytes(&mut nonce);

    // AAD = export_id || slot_id (binds slot to this export)
    let mut aad = Vec::with_capacity(20);
    aad.extend_from_slice(export_id);
    aad.extend_from_slice(&slot_id.to_le_bytes());

    let wrapped = cipher.encrypt(
        Nonce::from_slice(&nonce),
        Payload { msg: dek, aad: &aad },
    )?;

    Ok((wrapped, nonce))
}

/// Unwrap DEK by trying each slot
pub fn unwrap_dek(
    secret: &[u8],
    export_id: &[u8; 16],
    slots: &[KeySlot],
) -> Result<Zeroizing<[u8; 32]>, CryptoError> {
    for slot in slots {
        // Derive KEK based on slot type
        let kek = match slot.kdf.as_str() {
            "argon2id" => derive_kek_argon2id(
                secret,
                &slot.salt,
                slot.kdf_params.as_ref().unwrap(),
            )?,
            "hkdf-sha256" => derive_kek_hkdf(secret, &slot.salt)?,
            _ => continue,
        };

        // Try unwrapping
        let cipher = Aes256Gcm::new(Key::from_slice(&*kek));
        let mut aad = Vec::with_capacity(20);
        aad.extend_from_slice(export_id);
        aad.extend_from_slice(&slot.id.to_le_bytes());

        if let Ok(dek_bytes) = cipher.decrypt(
            Nonce::from_slice(&slot.nonce),
            Payload { msg: &slot.wrapped_dek, aad: &aad },
        ) {
            let mut dek = Zeroizing::new([0u8; 32]);
            dek.copy_from_slice(&dek_bytes);
            return Ok(dek);
        }
        // Auth tag mismatch → try next slot
    }

    Err(CryptoError::InvalidPassword)
}
```

### Key Management CLI Commands

```
cass pages key list    --archive ./site
cass pages key add     --archive ./site --password "current" --new-password "new"
cass pages key revoke  --archive ./site --password "valid" --slot-id 2
cass pages key rotate  --archive ./site --old-password "old" --new-password "new"
```

### config.json Key Slots Section

```json
{
    "key_slots": [
        {
            "id": 0,
            "slot_type": "password",
            "kdf": "argon2id",
            "kdf_params": {"memory_kb": 65536, "iterations": 3, "parallelism": 4},
            "salt": "base64...",
            "nonce": "base64...",
            "wrapped_dek": "base64..."
        },
        {
            "id": 1,
            "slot_type": "recovery",
            "kdf": "hkdf-sha256",
            "salt": "base64...",
            "nonce": "base64...",
            "wrapped_dek": "base64..."
        }
    ]
}
```

### Test Cases

1. Create slot → unwrap with same password works
2. Unwrap with wrong password → error
3. Multiple slots → any valid secret works
4. Add slot → new password works
5. Revoke slot → old password fails
6. Rotate → old password fails, new works
7. AAD tampering → decryption fails

## Files to Create/Modify

- `src/pages/keyslot.rs` (new)
- `src/pages/encrypt.rs` (integrate slots)
- `src/cli/pages.rs` (key management commands)
- `tests/pages_keyslot.rs` (new)

## Exit Criteria

1. Multiple slots work independently
2. Add/revoke operations modify only config.json
3. Rotate re-encrypts payload with new DEK
4. AAD binding prevents cross-export attacks
5. All key management commands work
6. Comprehensive test coverage","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T01:32:39.857833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T15:52:18.257344Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T15:52:18.257344Z","close_reason":"Implemented in src/pages/encrypt.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-mlou","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-x9fd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
